commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the embedded runner orphaned-user prompt marker and aligned tests/helpers so older queued user text is framed as prior-turn context before the active prompt.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests -1. Total +1 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the deterministic prompt assembly shape: source shows current `main` prepends older orphaned user text before the latest prompt, and the PR body shows the after-fix helper output. No live provider reproduction of the stochastic stale visible reply was established in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers should decide whether to accept the prompt and SDK test-contract string change, then proceed through normal merge checks.

target.title: fix #76888: [Bug]: Queued/orphaned user-message merge can produce stale reply
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #76888.
- Keeps orphaned queued user text in the embedded runner prompt, but labels it as prior-turn context instead of another active instruction to answer.
- Adds focused merge-strategy coverage for the latest triggering prompt staying authoritative after orphan repair.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `mergeOrphanedTrailingUserPrompt(...)` preserved an orphaned trailing user leaf by prepending it to the next run prompt under a neutral queued-message marker.
- That preserved the text and removed the duplicate transcript leaf, but it left the older user turn visually positioned before the latest triggering prompt without telling the model that the older text was context-only.
- In a queued/channel-backed run, that shape could let the older unresolved turn steer the visible response even though the final submitted prompt text was the latest user instruction.

## Fix

- Change the orphaned-user merge marker to say the previous active-turn message is preserved as context only and that the active prompt follows below.
- Keep the existing leaf-removal behavior, structured-content summarization, duplicate detection, and role-ordering repair unchanged.
- Keep the public runtime-test contract helper aligned with the production marker so native runtime adapter contract tests assert the same behavior.

## Maintainer-ready details

- Behavior or issue addressed: Orphaned queued user text can be merged into a later embedded-agent prompt in a way that makes the prior user turn look like the current answer target.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The stale-reply risk comes from the repaired prompt shape, so the patch changes the model-consumed source-of-truth marker emitted by `mergeOrphanedTrailingUserPrompt(...)` before the prompt is sent to the model instead of adding downstream filtering or dropping preserved text.
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-merge-strategy.test.ts`, plus existing helper and context-engine orphan-repair coverage.
- Scenario locked in: A previous active-turn user leaf with `older active-turn message` is merged into a later `user` trigger with `newest inbound message`; the regression test asserts the older text is preserved as context only and the newest inbound message remains below the marker as the active prompt.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test locks the prompt-shape invariant at the message-merge strategy seam, which is the same source path used by the embedded runner before model feedback, without mocking provider wording or expanding channel delivery behavior.
- Fix classification: Minimal root-cause behavior fix in embedded-runner prompt assembly.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the deterministic prompt assembly behavior; live provider wording can still vary, so the PR does not claim a provider-level stochastic reproduction.
- Patch quality notes: No config, schema, protocol, storage, provider routing, queue semantics, public API, or channel delivery code changed. The `transcript-repair-runtime-contract.ts` edit is a test-helper contract mirror aligned to the production marker, not a second runtime path. Related open PR / competition scan: none found in the task evidence. Out of scope: no public API, schema, protocol, default, migration, or compatibility behavior is changed.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: When an orphaned queued user leaf is merged into a later embedded-agent run, the prompt now preserves the older text as context only and points the model back to the active prompt below, reducing the chance that the stale prior user turn becomes the answer target.
- Real environment tested: Linux task worktree executing the production `mergeOrphanedTrailingUserPrompt(...)` helper through `pnpm exec tsx`; this is the deterministic prompt-assembly path that creates the stale-reply risk before any provider call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx --eval 'import { mergeOrphanedTrailingUserPrompt } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.ts"; const result = mergeOrphanedTrailingUserPrompt({ prompt: "newest inbound message", trigger: "user", leafMessage: { content: "older active-turn message" } }); console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));'`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-76888-evidence/validation-runs/1780694626271-real-behavior-terminal-capture.log`:

```text
{
  "prompt": "[Queued user message from a previous active turn; preserved as context only. Continue with the active prompt below.]\nolder active-turn message\n\nnewest inbound message",
  "merged": true,
  "removeLeaf": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The production helper keeps `older active-turn message` exactly once, removes the orphan transcript leaf, and sends the model a repaired prompt where the marker says the older text is prior-turn context and `newest inbound message` remains the active prompt below it.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/channel-backed provider conversation or reproduce the original stochastic stale visible reply with a real model; the proof is focused on the deterministic embedded-runner prompt assembly path that creates the reported stale-reply risk.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target owner surface: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.ts` and the message-merge strategy seam.
- Added/updated `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-merge-strategy.test.ts` so the default strategy explicitly expects the context-only marker before the latest inbound message.
- Updated existing helper and runtime-contract expectations in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/transcript-repair-runtime-contract.test.ts` to keep production and contract fixtures aligned.
- Re-ran the existing context-engine orphan repair filter to prove the full embedded runner path still preserves orphan text, hides runtime-only context correctly, branches away the orphan leaf, and submits the repaired prompt.

## Merge risk

- Why it matters / User impact: A channel-backed agent should answer the latest triggering user instruction, not an earlier unresolved user turn that was only preserved to repair transcript ordering.
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk:behavior`, because the text sent to the model changes in a user-visible embedded-runner repair path.
- Risk explanation: This is intentionally a prompt-shape change, but it does not drop any user text. It only clarifies the role of the older orphaned text as context while keeping the latest prompt as the active instruction.
- What did NOT change: No config, schema, protocol, storage, provider routing, queue semantics, transcript leaf removal, structured content summarization, duplicate detection, or channel delivery behavior changed.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The source of truth stays in the embedded runner transcript repair helper and its message-merge strategy contract; callers still use the existing strategy seam and do not add a second merge path.
- Why acceptable: The change is the smallest owner-boundary fix for the deterministic prompt shape that ClawSweeper identified, with focused helper and runner-path tests and no new public API surface.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
